repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orth_uniform/v2: canonical-EIF/DML setup and ATE report frontier guardrail

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML and SemenovaChernozhukov2021DMLCATE: AIPW/DML orthogonal score identity and sufficient remainder control

repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3 has no ExactID DML frontier; eid_dml_orth_uniform/v2 concerns DML/LRO/ADML studentization reports, not Holder/entropy HTE remainder frontier

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML gives sufficient orthogonal-score product-rate inference; FosterSyrgkanis2023OSL gives excess-risk entropy bounds; SemenovaChernozhukov2021DMLCATE gives high-level simultaneous CATE inference; none gives this iff lower-bound frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3 has no ExactID DML Holder exponent frontier

published_prior_art.cite: KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE gives minimax CATE elbows; ArmstrongKolesar2021FiniteSampleATE gives honest finite-sample ATE inference; neither states a first-order DML HTE uniform-inference exponent boundary

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The §8 comparison to ArmstrongKolesar2021FiniteSampleATE is a bare citation with no section/page/equation anchor tying that paper to the claimed smoothness-overlap inference gap.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed second-order remainder uses denominators p0 and 1-p0 but the exact plug-in AIPW drift has denominators involving the estimated propensity, leaving an unaccounted higher-order term unless an extra condition such as sqrt(n)·delta_g·delta_p^2=o(1) is imposed.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The lower-bound half is quantified over nuisance-class radii and an active tangent cone, but it never states that the actual cross-fit learner errors attain those directions; an oracle or faster learner could have the same admissible class with zero visible remainder.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The iff necessity route only sketches failure when inf_P B_n(P)->infty, while the stated iff requires failure for the whole complement of sup_P B_n(P)->0, including bounded-away-from-zero and sparse worst-case sequences.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Rewrite Conjecture 1 with explicit minimax/adversarial quantifiers: specify whether the lower bound is for every first-order learner, for some admissible learner sequence, or for minimax risk over the Holder class.
Patch Theorem 1 by using the exact plug-in remainder with estimated propensity denominators, or add a named residual term plus rate condition controlling it.
Add a location-level anchor for ArmstrongKolesar2021FiniteSampleATE and, if possible, also cite MackeySyrgkanisZadik2018 as a limitations comparator for first- versus higher-order orthogonalization.
Strengthen §10 by giving a concrete route for the intermediate frontier case B_n(P)->c>0, not only the divergent-remainder case.

recommended_next_step: Revise the statement quantifiers and exact remainder algebra, then add the missing Armstrong-Kolesar location anchor before resubmitting.
